title: Kyle Klaudt | Partner | BAL Immigration Law
content:
“At BAL, we use technology to simplify the complex, allowing me to dedicate my time to the people behind the cases. I find purpose in building lasting relationships and helping my clients navigate their journeys to achieve their personal and professional goals.”
Kyle is a Partner in BAL’s Dallas office, serving as an important architect of BAL’s best practices and effective training resources. As a member of the Practice Management: Best Practices group, he is adept at developing creative solutions to challenges facing companies and creating resources that improve processes, increasing efficiency for both clients and the firm. Kyle is a member of one of the largest legal teams at BAL. He helped create a standard operating model for the team that streamlines the day-to-day casework. These legal strategies and process optimizations not only save clients significant expense but also save attorneys critical time. For example, Kyle created a system to automate responses to U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services requests for evidence for certain case types, saving his team more than 6,000 hours of preparation time in responding to the agency’s more than 2,000 requests. This allows his team to focus on client service and legal strategy versus administrative work. Additionally, the legal strategy frameworks that Kyle designed have drastically decreased requests for evidence on visa applications for clients and increased approval rates year over year. Kyle is a member of the American Immigration Lawyers Association, the Texas Young Lawyers Association and the Dallas Bar Association. He has consistently been recognized in the Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch list. In his free time, Kyle enjoys traveling, hiking and spending time with his family and two dogs.
Arizona Summit Law School, J.D. University of Montana, B.S., Finance
